title: Delete Columns to Make Sorted II

testcases.input: strs = ["ca","bb","ac"] Output: 1 Explanation: After deleting the first column, strs = ["a", "b", "c"]. Now strs is in lexicographic order (ie. strs[0] <= strs[1] <= strs[2]). We require at least 1 deletion since initially strs was not in lexicographic order, so the answer is 1.
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: After deleting the first column, strs = ["a", "b", "c"]. Now strs is in lexicographic order (ie. strs[0] <= strs[1] <= strs[2]). We require at least 1 deletion since initially strs was not in lexicographic order, so the answer is 1.

testcases.input: strs = ["xc","yb","za"] Output: 0 Explanation: strs is already in lexicographic order, so we do not need to delete anything. Note that the rows of strs are not necessarily in lexicographic order: i.e., it is NOT necessarily true that (strs[0][0] <= strs[0][1] <= ...)
testcases.expectedOutput: 0 Explanation: strs is already in lexicographic order, so we do not need to delete anything. Note that the rows of strs are not necessarily in lexicographic order: i.e., it is NOT necessarily true that (strs[0][0] <= strs[0][1] <= ...)

testcases.input: strs = ["zyx","wvu","tsr"] Output: 3 Explanation: We have to delete every column.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: We have to delete every column.
